reasoning: The FRED linear model for the 10-year real interest rate (REAINTRATREARAT10Y) points to a steady downward trend over the past year (from ~2.0% to ~1.6%), projecting a probability of increase of 0.30 over a 1-month horizon. While nominal 10-year Treasury yields stopped falling and bounced slightly back above 4.1% in late October/early November 2025, inflation has also remained somewhat sticky, potentially suppressing the real rate if nominal yields fail to rise proportionately. In light of the statistical projection and qualitative trends, a short-term increase in the published estimate is less likely than a continued flat or down drift. For the longer 3-month horizon out to February 2026, general uncertainty dictates reverting the probability more strongly toward the base rate (0.50).
